title: HAVING: showtimes with more than 2 seats
prompt_md: Find only the showtimes that have **more than 2** seats. Group `seats` by `showtime_id`, then filter the groups with `HAVING` (you can't use `WHERE` to filter an aggregate). Return `showtime_id` and the count aliased as `seat_count` (cast with `::int`).
starter_code:
SELECT showtime_id, COUNT(*)::int AS seat_count
FROM seats
GROUP BY showtime_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;
hints: `WHERE` filters individual rows before grouping; `HAVING` filters groups after.
Put the aggregate condition in `HAVING COUNT(*) > 2`.
Don't forget to alias the count as `seat_count` and cast it `::int`.
reference_solution: SELECT showtime_id, COUNT(*)::int AS seat_count FROM seats GROUP BY showtime_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;
